lastRunSummary: Session stopped: no completion signal after task 3

gameQueue.title: Iron Horse
gameQueue.concept: Steam locomotive driving puzzle. Manage boiler pressure by shoveling coal on the beat grid while the train accelerates through 5 stations. Too little pressure and you stall; too much and the boiler blows. Brake precisely at each station marker to pick up passengers and supplies. Hazards (rockslides, cattle on track, broken rails) require split-second speed corrections.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First steam locomotive simulation in series, first beat-grid resource management (coal shoveling on rhythm subdivisions), first precision-stop mechanic (brake-to-marker within tolerance), first game combining rhythm-based resource input with spatial stopping precision.

gameQueue.title: Bounty Board
gameQueue.concept: Strategic card-drafting western. Each round reveals 6 outlaw wanted posters with different bounty values and capture difficulties. Spend your limited posse (3 deputies with unique skills: tracker, gunfighter, negotiator) to claim posters. Rival marshal AI competes for the same targets. Capture difficulty scales with the outlaw's danger rating vs your assigned deputy's strength. Plan 5 rounds ahead — spent deputies need rest before reassignment.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First card-drafting / deck-builder mechanic in series, first multi-unit assignment puzzle (deputies as resource to allocate), first strategic multi-round planning game with rival AI competing for same targets.

gameQueue.title: Quicksand
gameQueue.concept: Timed physics rescue puzzle. A wagon has sunk into quicksand and livestock are scattered. Use a limited rope (drag to connect anchor points: trees, boulders, fence posts) to form a pulley system that multiplies force. The quicksand applies increasing suction over time. Each level has different anchor positions and vehicle weight — find the rope configuration that generates enough mechanical advantage before the wagon sinks completely.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First rope-as-tool physics puzzle in series, first mechanical advantage simulation (pulley force multiplication), first game where the solution is a spatial configuration rather than a reflex response.
